title: citrus medica var. aruensis
intro: Citrus medica var. aruensis, also known as Fingered Citron or Buddha's Hand, is a rare and unique variety of citron that is characterized by its finger-like or thumb-like protrusions. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antiviral properties, and is believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Citrus medica var. aruensis include extracts, essential oils, and teas, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits make it a valuable resource for those seeking natural remedies for a variety of health conditions.
